# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Package Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

env:
  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

jobs:
  build-and-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Node and npm
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'
          npm-auth-token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
          npm-email: ${{ secrets.NPM_EMAIL }}

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build the application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Create a GitHub release
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-releaser@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          prerelease: false
          draft: true
          release_name: 'Release {{ .GITHUB_REF }}'

      - name: Publish to npm/PyPI
        run: |
          npm publish --tag latest
          # pypi publication
          twine upload dist/*
